# pricefixed

> Open, dependency-free tools to pull apartment listings and price history straight from landlord-direct feeds into a local SQLite database. Built to be handed to an AI agent. NYC first. A transparency project against algorithmic rent price-fixing.

Python 3.9+ standard library only. Run `python3 scrape.py` to pull every source into `listings.db`. Every pull snapshots price and lease terms so history is preserved.

## Start here

- [BUILD.md](BUILD.md): the guided prompt — interview the user on what apartment database they want, then build it. Start here if a user asks you to build something.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how an AI agent should drive this repo, the data model, and common tasks
- [README.md](README.md): what this is and why it exists
- [COMPILE.md](COMPILE.md): the method for turning any source in the map into a working adapter, at scale
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): how to add a new landlord source in ~30 lines

## Reference

- [FEEDS.md](FEEDS.md): the registry of NYC apartment data sources, tiered by difficulty and by what each exposes
- [pricefixed/core.py](pricefixed/core.py): the framework, SQLite schema, and the `LISTING_FIELDS` an adapter returns
- [scrape.py](scrape.py): the live-listings CLI (`--list`, `--status`, `--source`)
- [build_record.py](build_record.py): builds the canonical NYC building record + history from NYC Open Data (`--list`, `--source`, `--limit`)
- [healthcheck.py](healthcheck.py): runs every feed and reports which are live
